name: File Metadata
description: Contextual information about a file, including attributes such as the file's name, size, type, content (e.g., signatures, headers, media), user/owner, permissions, timestamps, and other related properties. File metadata provides insights into a file's characteristics and can be used to detect malicious activity, unauthorized modifications, or other anomalies.

metadata_types.description: Extracting headers to verify signature or detect packing/obfuscation.
Monitoring files located in unusual directories or paths (e.g., temporary or user folders).

event_logs.description: A network share object was accessed

powershell.command: Get-ChildItem -Path "C:\Path\To\Directory" -Recurse | Select-Object Name, Length, LastWriteTime, Attributes
powershell.description: Retrieve metadata about file access and permissions.

audit_framework.description: Configure audit rules to log metadata access.

siem_integration.action: Forward file metadata logs from endpoint or network devices to a SIEM for centralized analysis.

log_sources.Name: auditd:CONFIG_CHANGE
log_sources.Channel: chmod or chown of hook files indicating privilege escalation or execution permission change

log_sources.Name: auditd:PATH
log_sources.Channel: file path modifications on critical system directories (/etc, /usr/bin, /usr/sbin, /var, /opt)

log_sources.Name: auditd:SYSCALL
log_sources.Channel: Inotify watch creation or auditctl changes on /etc/cron* or /lib/systemd/system/

log_sources.Name: auditd:SYSCALL
log_sources.Channel: syscall in (chmod, fchmod, fchmodat, chown, fchown, fchownat, setxattr, lsetxattr, fsetxattr)

log_sources.Name: auditd:SYSCALL
log_sources.Channel:
syscall in (chmod,

log_sources.Name: auditd:SYSCALL
log_sources.Channel: chmod, chown, setxattr, or file writes to /etc/ssl/* or /usr/local/share/ca-certificates/*
